title: Sentinel Shield immutable source-acquisition record
description: Shape of the .sentinel-shield-ref record written by scripts/acquire-sentinel-shield.sh next to an acquired immutable checkout. It captures the NORMALIZED, privacy-preserving provenance of the source (repository kind, ref, resolved commit) plus the OPTIONAL source-verification outcome. Contains NO secrets, tokens, credentials, or local/home paths: a local-path source records repository=null and credential userinfo is never persisted. All verification fields are additive; readers that ignore them are unaffected. The verification contract is EXPLICIT: tree-record RECORDS a computed HEAD tree id (never a comparison); tree-checksum COMPARES the computed tree to a required expected tree and records BOTH; signature verifies a signed annotated tag AND its target commit. A calculated-but-uncompared value is NEVER labelled verified.

repository_kind.description: How the source was named: 'github' (owner/repo shorthand), 'url' (explicit remote, sanitized), or 'local' (a local path, whose value is NEVER persisted).

repository.description: Normalized provenance: owner/repo for github, a sanitized URL (no userinfo/query/fragment) for url, and null for a local path. MUST NOT carry credential userinfo.

ref.description: The immutable ref that was acquired: an annotated/lightweight tag name, or a full 40-hex commit SHA. Moving branches are refused at acquisition and never recorded.

resolved_commit.description: The full 40-hex commit SHA the checkout HEAD resolved to.

ref_kind.description: Authoritative classification of 'ref' at acquisition time — the immutability proof doctor.sh relies on. 'tag' or 'sha' only (a name is never trusted as a tag without this).

verification_method.description: Which OPTIONAL source verification ran. 'none' = disabled. 'tree-record' = the HEAD tree id was RECORDED but compared to nothing (not a verification). 'tree-checksum' = the HEAD tree was compared EXACTLY to a caller-supplied expected tree (fail closed). 'signature' = a signed annotated tag was verified (GPG or SSH per Git config) and its target commit confirmed. Combined mode requires both.

tree_expected.description: The caller-supplied expected HEAD tree object id. Present ONLY for tree-checksum modes — an uncompared tree-record NEVER records an expectation.

tree_calculated.description: The deterministic HEAD tree object id (git rev-parse HEAD^{tree}) computed from the checkout. Present for any tree mode. For tree-checksum it equals tree_expected (a mismatch fails closed and writes no record).

signature_status.description: Signature outcome for signature modes. Only 'good' is ever recorded — an unverifiable/bad signature fails closed and writes no record.

signature_mechanism.description: Best-effort classification of the mechanism git used to verify the tag (GPG status lines vs SSH), or 'unknown' when not determinable. Derived without echoing signer identity or key paths.

tag_object.description: The annotated tag OBJECT id (refs/tags/<ref>) that was signature-verified.

peeled_commit.description: The COMMIT the verified signed tag peels to (refs/tags/<ref>^{commit}); it must equal resolved_commit (identity is never bypassed by a good signature).
